title: styphelia stomarrhena
intro: Styphelia stomarrhena, also known as Tasmanian styphelia, is a shrub native to Australia, specifically in the southeastern region of Tasmania. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Styphelia stomarrhena has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, and its extracts have been used to treat wounds and skin conditions. The herbal preparations of Styphelia stomarrhena typically involve infusions or decoctions of the plant, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
